questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
At constant T and $\mathrm{P}, 5.0 \mathrm{~L}$ of $\mathrm{SO}_2$ are reacted with 3.0 L of $\mathrm{O}_2$ according to the following equation $2 \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{SO}_3(\mathrm{~g})$
The volume of the reaction mixture at the completion of the reaction is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: 1.250 g of metal carbonate $\left(\mathrm{MCO}_3\right)$ was treated with 500 mL of 0.1 M HCl solution. The unreacted HCl required 50.0 mL of 0.500 M NaOH solution for neutralization. Identify the metal M

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Battery acid $\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4\right)$ has density $1.285 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3} \cdot 10.0 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of this acid is diluted to $1 \mathrm{~L}$. $25 .0 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of this diluted solution requires $25.0 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of 0.1 N sodium hydroxide solution for neutralization. The percentage of sulphuric acid by mass in the battery acid is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Lithium oxide ( $\mathrm{Li}_2 \mathrm{O}$; molar mass $=30 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) is used in space shuttles to remove water vapour according to the following reaction
$\mathrm{Li}_2 \mathrm{O}_{(\mathrm{s})}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{LiOH}_{(\mathrm{s})}$
If 60 kg of water and 45 kg of $\mathrm{Li}_2 \mathrm{O}$ are present in a shuttle
I. water will be removed completely
II. $\mathrm{Li}_2 \mathrm{O}$ will be the limiting reagent
III. 100 kg of $\mathrm{Li}_2 \mathrm{O}$ will be required to completely remove the water present
IV. 27 kg of water will remain in the shuttle at the end of the reaction

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A fuel/oxidant system consisting of $\mathrm{N}, \mathrm{N}$-dimethylhydrazine $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{NNH}_2$ and $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$ (both liquids) is used in space vehicle propulsion. The liquid components are mixed stoichiometrically so that $\mathrm{N}_2, \mathrm{CO}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ are the only products. If all gases are under the same reaction conditions, number of moles of gases produced from 1 mole of $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{NNH}_2$ is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Which of the following mixtures of water and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ would have mass percentage of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ close to $30 ?$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Which one of the following statements is not correct about glucose?
$\left(\right.$ molar mass of glucose $\left.=180 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$
options: An aqueous 0.25 M solution of glucose is prepared by dissolving 45 g of glucose in water to give $1000 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of solution
1.00 mmol glucose has a mass of 180 mg
90.0 g glucose contain $1.8 \times 10^{22}$ atoms of carbon
$100 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of a 0.10 M solution contains 1.8 g of glucose

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: A student performed an experiment to determine the molecular formula of a given sample of hydrated copper (II) sulphate by weighing the sample before and after heating. The formula obtained experimentally was $\mathrm{CuSO}_4 5.5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ while the actual formula of the given sample is $\mathrm{CuSO}_4 \cdot 5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. Which experimental error would account for the wrongly obtained result?
options: During heating, some of the hydrated copper(II) sulphate was lost
The hydrated sample was not heated long enough to remove all the water present
Weight of the hydrated sample recorded was less than the actual weight taken
The balance used in the study showed all weights consistently high by 0.10 g

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Sodium azide $\left(\mathrm{NaN}_3\right)$ is used in the airbag of cars. This is a safety device which inflates on an impact according to the reaction $2 \mathrm{NaN}_3 \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}+3 \mathrm{~N}_2$
An air bag of a particular car can be filled with 44.8 L of gas at STP. The mass $(\mathrm{g})$ of $\mathrm{NaN}_3$ required to fill this airbag completely at 298 K and 1 atm . pressure is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
A 500 mL glass flask is filled at 298 K and 1 atm . pressure with three diatomic gases $X, Y$ and $Z$. The initial volume ratio of the gases before mixing was $5: 3: 1$. The density of the heaviest gas in the mixture is not more than 25 times that of the lightest gas. When the mixture was heated, vigorous reactions take place between X and Y and $X$ and $Z$ in which all the three gases were completely used up.
The gases $X, Y, Z$ respectively are

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
'Iodine number' is the grams of iodine atoms (atomic mass $=127 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) that can react completely with100 g of a vegetable oil. Iodine monochloride (ICl) is a reagent used to determine iodine number. In an experiment, $25.00 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of $0.100 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{dm}{ }^{-3}$ ICl was added to 127 g of the oil. The unreacted ICl was found to be equivalent to $40.00 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ of $0.10 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{dm}{ }^{-3}$ of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$.
The iodine number of the oil can be deduced as

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Myoglobin, (Mb), an oxygen storage protein, contains $0.34 \% \mathrm{Fe}$ by mass and in each molecule of myoglobin one ion of Fe is present. Molar mass of $\mathrm{Mb}\left(\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right)$ is $\left(\right.$ Molar mass of $\left.\mathrm{Fe}=55.845 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
Which of the following statement/s is/are correct?
I. Number of significant figures in 2345.100 is three
II. 0.00787 rounded to two significant figures is written as $0.787 \times 10^{-2}$
III. 340 rounded to two significant figures is written as $0.34 \times 10^3$
IV. The number of significant figures in 0.020 is two

questions.q: A common method to clean acid spills is to use $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ (Molar mass 106 g ). If 50.0 mL of 0.75 M HCl is spilt on a wooden surface, the amount of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ required is

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
The analysis of three different binary oxides of bromine $(\mathrm{Br})$ and oxygen $(\mathrm{O})$ gives the following results:
Compound Mass of O combined with 1.0 g of Br
$\mathbf{X}$ 0.101 g
$\mathbf{Y}$ 0.303 g
$\mathbf{Z}$ 0.503 g
Which of the following statements is not correct? I Compound $\mathbf{Y}$ is $\mathrm{Br}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ II Compound $\mathbf{Z}$ is $\mathrm{Br}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ III Compound $\mathbf{Z}$ is $\mathrm{Br}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ IV Compound $\mathbf{Y}$ is $\mathrm{Br}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$

questions.q: Mercury is highly hazardous and hence its concentration is expressed in the units of ppb (micrograms of Hg present in 1 L of water). Permissible level of Hg in drinking water is 0.0335 ppb . Which of the following is an alternate representation of this concentration?

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: Antacids are medicines that temporarily neutralize the acid in the stomach and prevent heartburns. The volume of an antacid syrup containing 2.9 g of $\mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ per 100 mL to be given to a patient whose stomach contains 2 L of gastric juice with HCl concentration of $6.0 \times 10^{-3} M$ is : $\left(\right.$ Molar mass of $\left.\mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2=58.0 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$
